2605.09769.title: UTS at PsyDefDetect: Multi-Agent Councils and Absence-Based Reasoning for Defense Mechanism Classification
2605.09769.paper_summary: This paper describes our system for classifying psychological defense mechanisms in emotional support dialogues using the Defense Mechanism Rating Scales (DMRS), placing second (F1 0.406) among 64 teams.1 A central insight is that defense mechanisms are defined by what is absent: missing affect, blocked cognition, denied reality. We encode this as an affect-cognition integration spectrum in prompt-level clinical rules, which account for the largest single gain (+11.4pp F1). Our architecture is a multi-phase deliberative council of Gemini 2.5 agents where class-specific advocates rate evidence strength rather than voting, achieving F1 0.382 with no fine-tuning - a top-5 result on its own. We find, however, that the council is confidently wrong about minority classes: 59-80% of stable minority predictions are incorrect, driven by a systematic "L7 attractor" in which emotional content defaults to the majority class. A targeted override ensemble from three fine-tuned Qwen3.5 models applies 16 overrides (+2.4pp), selected by a structured multi-agent system (builder, critic, regression guard) that produced a larger F1 gain in one iteration than 8 prior attempts combined.
2605.09769.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文描述了我們的系統，使用防衛機制評分量表（DMRS）對情感支持對話中的心理防衛機制進行分類，在64支隊伍中名列第二（F1 0.406）。<lb/>一個核心見解是，防衛機制是由缺失的東西來定義的：缺失的情感、阻塞的認知、否認的現實。<lb/>我們將這一點編碼為一個情感-認知整合光譜，在提示級臨床規則中，這占據了最大的單一增益（+11.4pp F1）。<lb/>我們的架構是一個多階段的Gemini 2.5代理的深思熟慮的委員會，其中類別特定的倡導者評估證據的強度，而不是投票，實現了F1 0.382，且無需微調——這本身就是前五名的結果。<lb/>然而，我們發現，委員會對少數類別的判斷自信卻錯誤：穩定的少數預測中有59-80%是錯誤的，這是由於一個系統性的“L7吸引子”，在這種情況下，情感內容默認為主要類別。<lb/>來自三個微調過的Qwen3.5模型的針對性覆蓋集應用了16個覆蓋（+2.4pp），這些覆蓋由一個結構化的多代理系統（建構者、批評者、回歸守護者）選擇，該系統在一次迭代中產生的F1增益超過了之前8次嘗試的總和。
2605.09769.author: Dima Galat et.al.
